title: lithocarpus andersonii
intro: Lithocarpus andersonii, also known as Anderson's tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree belonging to the beech family, Fagaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which is used to treat various health conditions. The bark of Lithocarpus andersonii contains tannins, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in herbal remedies to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
